title: Robust Dense Egyptian Macaroni Pasta
description: A peppery, dense Egyptian dish featuring macaroni, perfect for a post-workout meal, this recipe is low-fat-friendly and packed with flavor. It combines the heartiness of macaroni with the richness of Egyptian spices.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the macaroni according to package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in the ground cumin, smoked paprika, black pepper, and salt, cooking for 1 minute to allow the spices to bloom.
Add the tomato paste to the skillet, stirring to combine with the spice mixture, and cook for 2 minutes.
Gradually add the vegetable broth to the skillet, whisking continuously to avoid lumps, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the sauce simmer for 10 minutes, allowing it to thicken and reduce slightly.
Drain the cooked macaroni and add it to the skillet with the sauce, tossing to combine and ensure the macaroni is well coated.
Serve the macaroni hot, garnished with additional smoked paprika if desired.
